deductions.claim: INPUT terminals: PE, U1/L, V1/N, W1, BRK+, BRK-
deductions.evidence: In the image the "INPUT" header bracket sits above only PE/U1-L/V1-N/W1; BRK+ and BRK- are drawn to the right of a separate ground-symbol icon and are not enclosed under the "INPUT" label bracket. They are the drive's brake-resistor/DC-bus terminals, a distinct group from the mains INPUT terminals, even though both sit on the same side of the drive box.
deductions.correction: List BRK+/BRK- separately as the drive's brake-resistor/common-DC terminals rather than folding them into "INPUT terminals."

deductions.claim: OUTPUT terminals: U2, V2, W2 [... connects to] Motor
deductions.evidence: The image shows a second, distinct set of terminal labels "U1 V1 W1" printed directly on the motor circle symbol (separate from the drive's own INPUT U1/L-V1/N-W1 and OUTPUT U2/V2/W2 labels). The candidate response never mentions these motor-side terminal labels at all, despite the label reuse (U1/V1/W1) being a legible and potentially confusing detail on the page.
deductions.correction: Note that drive OUTPUT (U2, V2, W2) connects via the motor cable to the motor's own terminals, labeled U1, V1, W1 on the motor.

deductions.claim: (omission) — grounding/PE terminals treated as a single generic "PE"
deductions.evidence: The image shows ground/earth symbol icons at two points in the drive box outline (between W1 and BRK+ on the input side, and to the right of W2 on the output side), plus a separate "PE" label with footnotes 6) and 7) at the bottom-left of the diagram describing a second grounding-conductor path. The response collapses all of this into one undifferentiated "grounding (PE)" requirement.
deductions.correction: Mention the drive's internal ground terminals (input and output) and the separate line-side PE/grounding-conductor path called out by footnotes 6) and 7).

deductions.claim: (omission) — power path described as supply -> drive INPUT -> drive OUTPUT -> motor with no mention of a disconnect device
deductions.evidence: Three switch/disconnect symbols (with footnote marker "5)") are drawn in series between the supply terminals (L1/L, L2/N, L3/-) and the drive INPUT terminals (U1/L, V1/N, W1), indicating a supply disconnecting device ahead of the drive. The candidate's power-flow description skips this element entirely.
deductions.correction: Note the supply disconnecting device (switches, footnote 5) shown in series ahead of the drive's INPUT terminals.

deductions.claim: "Nothing major appears to be unreadable in the provided image."
deductions.evidence: The image is in fact legible, but this blanket completeness claim glosses over several clearly-legible elements the response never addresses: the motor-side U1/V1/W1 terminal labels, the ground symbols, the disconnect-switch symbols, and the seven numbered footnotes (1-7) carrying substantive installation requirements (e.g. 360-degree shield grounding, one-phase wiring). Asserting nothing is unreadable, without engaging with content that plainly is readable, implies a completeness the answer doesn't back up.
deductions.correction: Either work the numbered footnotes and the motor/ground labels into the explanation, or explicitly scope the answer as covering only the main power-terminal path rather than implying full coverage.

deductions.claim: (omission) — L1/L, L2/N, L3/- discussed with no mention of the one-phase installation note
deductions.evidence: Footnote "4)" directly beneath the L1/L, L2/N, L3/- labels states: "In one-phase installations, connect phase to L, neutral to N, leave L3/- disconnected." This is a legible, directly relevant caveat on the exact terminals the response already discusses, and omitting it reduces the practical usefulness of the explanation for a technician wiring a single-phase supply.
deductions.correction: Include the one-phase wiring note (footnote 4) since it directly qualifies the L1/L, L2/N, L3/- terminals already discussed.

notes: Clean power/connection diagram (ABB ACS355 manual p.50-51), no control/interlock logic present, so sequence_interlocks_logic scores full since the candidate invents no false sequence/interlock content. All identifiers the candidate asserted (PE, U1/L, V1/N, W1, BRK+, BRK-, U2, V2, W2, L1/L, L2/N, L3/-) are genuinely legible in the image -- no invented tags or components, so no hard failure. Deductions are for omissions of other clearly-legible, technically relevant content (motor-side terminal labels, disconnect switches, ground symbols, footnotes) and one grouping inaccuracy (BRK+/BRK- called INPUT terminals), not for false claims.
